meta.phase-name: Visual composer reusable blocks, dynamic data, and containers

problem.item: The builder is not complete until reusable blocks, pre-wired dynamic blocks, simple containers, and `Custom HTML` move from taxonomy/planning into real content capabilities.
These features need careful constraints so the system gains power without becoming a confusing generic no-code platform.

goals.goal: Support reusable block insertion with explicit linked-vs-copy behavior.
Implement the first pre-wired dynamic block set for Grime Time rather than a generic query builder.
Support a constrained first container model with named slots and nesting validation.
Add a real `Custom HTML` block with clear labeling and an explicit safe-rendering strategy.

non-goals.item: Do not ship arbitrarily deep freeform nesting.
Do not build a fully generic query-builder UI for dynamic blocks.
Do not treat raw custom HTML execution as solved without a documented safety decision.

definition-of-done.item: Reusable blocks can be inserted as linked references or detached copies with clear labeling.
The first dynamic blocks are powered by Grime Time-specific data bindings and show representative preview states.
Supported container blocks expose named slots, child insertion, and structural validation.
`Custom HTML` exists as a real block with content editing, preview strategy, and safety notes.

tasks.task: Define and implement reusable-block insertion semantics, including linked reference vs detached copy behavior.
Add the first pre-wired dynamic blocks and data-source editors for Grime Time content surfaces.
Implement the first constrained container blocks with slots, child insertion, and nesting validation.
Add the `Custom HTML` block to the registry, editor, preview, and structure labeling path, with a documented safe-rendering decision.
Add verification for reusable, dynamic, container, and custom-HTML flows.
